  "rewrite": "Support DLedger Controller Snapshot\n# Background\nThe DLedger Controller mode was launched after RocketMQ 5.0, which can provide the function of automatic master-slave switching.\n\nThe principle is as follows:\n\nDLedger Controller is a strongly consistent metadata center built on DLedger (Raft), which stores metadata related to master selection.\n\nHowever, because DLedger did not provide the ability of Snapshot before, when the Controller restarts, it needs to replay all the logs in DLedger to restore the state before Shutdown. Restarting all logs can cause the restart to take a lot of time, especially when there are a lot of logs.\n\nRecently, we added the Snapshot capability to DLedger, thanks to @tsunghanjacktsai for his contribution.\n\nThe basic principle is as follows: For example, for the log before LogIndex = 6, DLedger will create a snapshot for it. This Snapshot contains the state machine state before LogIndex = 6. In this way, when restarting, we only need to load this snapshot, and only r",
",

}

  "rewrite": "Function `x0x1_after_extraction` should update the extracted array's regions by mapping each region from their coordinates on the original array to the extracted array. This function compares the 1D coordinates of a region's original coordinates on the array to the 1D coordinates of the extracted array, determining where the original region lies on the extracted array. For example, for a 1D array with shape 8, we may have a region whose 1D coordinates are (2, 3) on the original array, and its 1D coordinates on the extracted array are (1, 2).",
 This function should return the 1D coordinates of the region on the extracted array.",
  "needs_human_review": false
}

  ],
  "overall_quality": "acceptable | needs_revision | poor",
  "rewrite": "Function `get_number_shards` should return the number of shards for `num_examples` of `total_size` in bytes. Each shard should be at least 128MB. A pod has 1616=256 TPU devices containing 1024 TPU chips (2048 cores). So if the dataset is large enough, we want the number of shards to be a multiple of 1024, but with shards as big as possible. If the dataset is too small, we want the number of shards to be a power of two so it distributes better on smaller TPU configs (8, 16, 32, ... cores).",
",
  "needs_human_review": false
}
